_publications._publicationTitle: Modelling neurofibromatosis type 1 tibial dysplasia and its treatment with lovastatin.

_contextSnippet: This was paralleled by an increased serum D-PYD in Nf1Prx1 animals, which was only slightly reduced by lovastatin treatment (control 2.5 ± 0.39 nMol/l; Nf1Prx1 3.95 ± 0.71 nMol/l
details: Increased serum D-PYD concentrations (3.95 ± 0.71 nMol/l) compared to controls (2.5 ± 0.39 nMol/l), indicating increased bone turnover
